item.title: KPeek 1.2.0
item.description:
What's New in KPeek 1.2.0

Apple-Native UI Refresh

  • Native sidebar with translucent material and system selection
  • System accent color throughout the app
  • Light and dark mode adaptive app icon
  • Native segmented pickers for all tab bars

Stat Cards

  • Topic Browser: partitions, retention, cleanup, compression
  • Message Viewer: message count, partitions, avg size, time span
  • Consumer Groups: total, stable, empty, dead counts
  • Consumer Group Detail: state, members, topics, partitions, total lag

JSON Syntax Highlighting

  • Color-coded JSON in message detail (keys, strings, numbers, booleans)
  • Colors adapt automatically to light/dark mode

Live Consumer Group Lag

  • Auto-refresh lag data every 5 seconds with Live toggle
  • Lag chart shows all partitions with red for lagging ones

Message Viewer Improvements

  • Hover highlighting on message rows
  • Debounced JSONPath filter for smooth typing
  • Duplicate detail window prevention

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed crash when switching clusters

item.title: KPeek 1.2.1
item.description:
KPeek v1.2.1

Lightweight native macOS Kafka message viewer. Self-contained .app bundle — no dependencies required.

Install

1. Download KPeek.zip 2. Unzip and drag KPeek.app to /Applications 3. Right-click > Open on first launch (ad-hoc signed)

Requirements

  • macOS 15.0+ (Apple Silicon)

Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action | |----------|--------| | Cmd+R | Refresh topics | | Cmd+Return | Start consuming | | Cmd+. | Stop consuming | | Cmd+K | Clear messages | | Cmd+Shift+E | Export messages as JSON | | Cmd+Shift+W | Disconnect |

item.title: KPeek 1.2.2
item.description:
KPeek v1.2.2

Lightweight native macOS Kafka message viewer. Self-contained .app bundle — no dependencies required.

Install

1. Download KPeek.zip 2. Unzip and drag KPeek.app to /Applications 3. Right-click > Open on first launch (ad-hoc signed)

Requirements

  • macOS 15.0+ (Apple Silicon)

Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action | |----------|--------| | Cmd+R | Refresh topics | | Cmd+Return | Start consuming | | Cmd+. | Stop consuming | | Cmd+K | Clear messages | | Cmd+Shift+E | Export messages as JSON | | Cmd+Shift+W | Disconnect |

item.description:

Install

1. Download KPeek.zip 2. Unzip and drag KPeek.app to /Applications 3. Right-click > Open on first launch (ad-hoc signed)

KPeek v1.2.5

item.title: KPeek 1.2.8
item.description:
What's New

  • Export/import connections as KPeek JSON or Offset Explorer XML
  • Connection picker for selecting which connections to import/export
  • Duplicate name resolution on import — appends (1), (2) etc.
  • Import now supports both JSON and XML formats
  • Dynamic release notes generated from commit log

item.title: KPeek 1.2.10
item.description:
What's New

  • Merge pull request #10 from dhpatel02/feature/topic-browser-nav-buttons
  • add View and Produce buttons to Topic Browser detail panel

Install

1. Download KPeek.zip 2. Unzip and drag KPeek.app to /Applications 3. Right-click > Open on first launch (ad-hoc signed) Requires macOS 15.0+ (Apple Silicon)

item.title: KPeek 1.2.11
item.description:
What's New

  • Improve consumer group lag performance with watermark failure tracking
  • Add warning banners for empty/dead consumer groups
  • Batch lag scan with bounded concurrency for stability
  • Optimize empty group fallback using cached consumer connections
  • Add colorful View/Produce buttons in Topic Browser
  • Increase minimum window size to 1280x700 for better layout
  • Right-align topic properties with truncation and hover tooltips
  • Remove unused dead code

item.title: KPeek 1.2.12
item.description:
What's New

  • optimize responsiveness, fix large message lag, and ad-hoc sign build
  • Replace O(n log n) full re-sort in flushToUI with O(n) linear merge
  • Cache filteredMessages once per body evaluation (avoid double computation)
  • Lazy-cache headers, hexValue on KafkaMessage (like prettyValue)
  • Reuse single-threaded Tokio runtime instead of creating per admin op
  • Change CLIENT from Mutex to RwLock for concurrent read access
  • Tune rdkafka prefetch buffers (1000 msgs, 64MB) and adaptive poll timeout
  • Fix large message detail lag: remove NSTextView from SwiftUI ScrollView,
  • enable non-contiguous layout, skip syntax highlighting above 512KB
  • Ad-hoc codesign dylibs and app bundle after install_name_tool rewrites
  • to fix SIGKILL (Code Signature Invalid) crash on macOS Tahoe
  • Add production documentation across all Rust and Swift modules
  • Improve release notes generation and update v1.2.11 appcast
  • Improve consumer group lag, UI polish, and dead code cleanup
  • Add watermark failure tracking with N/A display in lag table
  • Optimize empty group fallback using cached consumer
  • Batch lag scan with bounded concurrency (50 threads)
  • Add warning banners for empty/dead groups and watermark timeouts
  • Disable batch scan lag feature behind flag for stability
  • Colorful View/Produce buttons in Topic Browser
  • Increase min window size to 1280x700
  • Right-align topic properties with truncation and hover tooltip
  • Remove unused kpeek_connect, kpeek_is_connected, MessageConsumer._topic

item.title: KPeek 1.2.13
item.description:
What's New

  • Add pagination support
  • Signed-off-by: anevtiaatd <126307746+anevtiaatd@users.noreply.github.com>
  • drag-to-reorder connections, theme switching, and smooth cluster switch
  • Add drag-and-drop reorder for connections on the connect screen
  • with native drag image, blue drop indicator line, and animated reorder
  • Add Dark/Light/System theme toggle via View menu, persisted with AppStorage
  • Soften light theme with off-white background tint
  • Fix message detail view backgrounds in light mode (remove low-opacity overlays)
  • Fix cluster switch flashing home screen: add switchCluster() that
  • disconnects internally without setting isConnected=false

item.title: KPeek 1.2.14
item.description:
What's New

  • Add check for auto update on app launch
  • Signed-off-by: anevtiaatd <126307746+anevtiaatd@users.noreply.github.com>

item.title: KPeek 1.2.16
item.description:
What's New

  • Add background lag monitoring with macOS notifications
  • Pin up to 10 consumer groups for background lag monitoring (bell icon per group row)
  • Background polling every 30s checks lag for all pinned groups
  • macOS native notifications when total lag exceeds user-defined threshold (default 1000)
  • Right-click bell icon to set custom lag threshold per group
  • Notification click navigates directly to the group's detail popup
  • Sortable columns on lag table (Topic, Partition, Committed, End, Lag)
  • Auto-sort by lag descending during live mode
  • Pin count indicator and Clear All button in bottom bar
  • 5-minute notification cooldown per group to prevent spam
  • Pinned groups persist across app restarts
  • Non-blocking app quit (Kafka disconnect runs in background)

item.title: KPeek 1.2.17
item.description:
What's New

  • Restyle lag monitoring controls with inline threshold editor
  • Inline editable threshold field per pinned group replacing right-click menu
  • Bell icon restyled as blue gradient button matching View button design
  • Button turns red when lag exceeds threshold
  • Fixed-width columns for consistent row alignment
  • Threshold field dismisses cursor on Enter

item.title: KPeek 1.3.0
item.description:
What's New

  • Remove redundant nil coalescing on non-optional subtitle
  • Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
  • address all critical, high, and medium severity review findings
  • Critical fixes:
  • Lag scan threads now use Arc-cloned fields instead of raw pointers,
  • eliminating use-after-free and unsound aliasing UB
  • Export XML documents credential exposure warning
  • High fixes:
  • Session poll releases mutex before 100ms broker poll (lock-free polling)
  • DefaultHasher replaced with deterministic FNV-1a for SSL cache keys
  • kpeek_update_filter wired to ConsumerSession.set_filter()
  • to_cstring replaces interior null bytes instead of silently truncating
  • catch_unwind added to kpeek_consumer_group_lag
  • SSL hostname verification documented as intentional trade-off
  • Medium fixes:
  • uuid_simple uses atomic counter for collision safety
  • parse_member_assignment caps loops at 10,000 iterations
  • RTF/tokenizer boolean checks use char slice comparison (O(1) vs O(n))
  • RTF Unicode escape handles U+8000+ with signed 16-bit two's complement
  • and supplementary characters via UTF-16 surrogate pairs
  • Scan mutex locks recover from poison instead of panicking
  • Lag scan group.id uses unique suffix to avoid cluster conflicts
  • Stale kpeek_format_value comment fixed
  • address critical and high-severity review findings
  • 1. Fix force-unwrap crash in prettyValue — use optional binding instead
  • of kpeek_format_json(ptr)! which segfaults if Rust returns null
  • 2. Add null ctx guards to kpeek_start_session, kpeek_session_poll,
  • kpeek_stop_session, kpeek_health_check, kpeek_get_metrics
  • 3. Replace cache.get().unwrap() with ok_or_else() in offset consumer
  • lookup to prevent panic on missing key
  • 4. Fix ctx use-after-free — check self?.ctx inside async blocks instead
  • of capturing raw pointer before dispatch
  • 5. Fix min_timestamp_ms sentinel — use i64::MAX/MIN instead of 0 to
  • correctly handle epoch-zero and negative timestamps
  • 6. Fix RTF highlighter char/byte index mismatch — use byte offset map
  • for safe string slicing with multi-byte Unicode characters
  • Update buttons to Tahoe UI style
  • Replace custom-styled buttons (manual padding, gradient backgrounds,
  • clip shapes) with native .borderedProminent + .controlSize(.small)
  • for Older/Newer pagination and View/Produce topic actions.
  • messages not streaming to UI during consumption
  • Two bugs fixed:
  • 1. Rust: initial fetch timeout/capacity check only ran on empty polls.
  • If messages flowed from some partitions but not all, the timeout
  • never fired and initial_fetch_done stayed false. Now checks on
  • every poll with messages too.
  • 2. Swift: during initial fetch phase, messages accumulated without
  • flushing to UI. Now flushes every 0.5s during initial fetch for
  • immediate visual feedback.
  • test: add Swift model tests (22) and UI smoke test automation
  • tests/swift/TestModels.swift: 22 tests for ConsumeMode FFI mapping,
  • merge sort (empty, interleave, stability, large arrays), array
  • chunking, and KafkaMessage model
  • tests/ui/test-ui-smoke.applescript: 7 UI tests via accessibility
  • (window existence, element count, menu bar, View menu, resize, crash)
  • tests/run-tests.sh: unified runner for Rust + Swift + optional UI
  • Total: 111 Rust + 22 Swift = 133 automated tests.
  • Run: ./tests/run-tests.sh (or --ui to include UI smoke tests)
  • test: add 45 more tests (111 total) covering edge cases and new modules
  • consumer.rs: 10 tests (MessageStats, SessionState, uuid_simple)
  • metrics.rs: 7 tests (counters, snapshots, concurrent access)
  • filter.rs: 14 edge cases (unicode, None values, deep nesting, booleans,
  • nulls, sort by offset/value, empty/single element, all-filtered-out)
  • offset_explorer.rs: 14 edge cases (JAAS parsing, whitespace trimming,
  • protocol mapping roundtrips, empty/multi export, SASL_PLAINTEXT)
  • test: add 66 unit tests for core modules and import
  • filter.rs: 16 tests (key/value matching, JSONPath, sorting, combined)
  • format.rs: 20 tests (lag, retention, time span, bytes, hex, quotes, JSON)
  • error.rs: 11 tests (codes, categories, retryability, anyhow classifier)
  • offset_explorer.rs: 12 tests (import, export, escaping, roundtrip)
  • All tests run without Kafka — pure logic validation via cargo test.
  • stop button now correctly stops consuming instead of restarting
  • The button action was always calling startConsuming(), with an onTapGesture
  • calling stopConsuming() — both fired simultaneously causing a restart.
  • Now the action toggles based on isConsuming state.
  • use withCString for topic in startConsuming to prevent use-after-free
  • The previous code used (topic as NSString).utf8String which returns an
  • inner pointer that may be invalidated before the FFI call. Now uses
  • topic.withCString to ensure the pointer is valid during the call.
  • Also captures maxMessageCount and keepOldest before the async block.
  • extract KPeekFFI wrapper and add RAII memory management
  • New KPeekFFI class provides type-safe Swift wrappers for all FFI calls,
  • hiding raw C pointer manipulation. ManagedCString ensures kpeek_free_*
  • is always called via deinit. KPeekBridge delegates consumer group, topic
  • management, producer, and topic config calls to KPeekFFI.
  • scripts/download-stats.sh queries GitHub release download counts and
  • logs to CSV for trend tracking. Run manually or via cron.
  • Add connection health check and internal metrics
  • Health check via kpeek_health_check() returns broker count or error.
  • Internal metrics track messages consumed/produced, bytes, poll count,
  • errors, and uptime with atomic counters for lock-free access.
  • Add Offset Explorer XML import/export in Rust
  • New import/offset_explorer.rs handles XML parsing and generation for
  • Offset Explorer connection configs. Includes JAAS config parsing,
  • security protocol mapping, and XML escaping. No external dependencies.
  • Add display formatting utilities in Rust
  • New core/format.rs with lag formatting (5.2M/1.3K), retention display
  • (7d/24h/∞), time span (5s/2m/1h), byte count, hex dump, smart quote
  • normalization, and JSON compaction. FFI exports as stateless functions.
  • move polling state machine from Swift to Rust ConsumerSession
  • New ConsumerSession wraps MessageConsumer with partition tracking, auto-stop,
  • initial fetch phase, and incremental stats. Swift pollLoop simplified from
  • ~180 lines to ~50 lines — just polls kpeek_session_poll() and flushes to UI.
  • Session handles idle detection, cap enforcement, and partition coverage.
  • Add message filter and sort engine in Rust
  • New core/filter.rs with FilterConfig (key/value substring, JSONPath),
  • SortConfig (5 fields, asc/desc), and filter_and_sort() entry point.
  • FFI CFilterConfig struct and kpeek_update_filter() stub added.
  • Full integration happens in Phase 2 (consumer session).
  • replace global FFI state with KPeekContext handles
  • Replace 4 global statics (CLIENT, CONSUMER, CONSUMER_REF, PRODUCER) and
  • 3 scan statics with a heap-allocated KPeekContext struct. All stateful FFI
  • functions now take a context pointer. Swift bridge holds OpaquePointer and
  • passes it to every call. Legacy connect/disconnect kept for compatibility.
  • Add structured error types with codes and categories
  • New KPeekError enum with 8 error variants, error codes matching
  • KPeekErrorCode C enum, and ErrorCategory (transient/permanent/config)
  • for UI-driven retry/prompt behavior. Includes anyhow::Error classifier.
  • Add type-safe C enums to FFI header
  • Replace magic integer comments with named enums: KPeekSecurityProtocol,
  • KPeekSaslMechanism, KPeekStartPosition, KPeekJsonTokenType. Integer
  • values unchanged — fully backwards compatible with existing Swift code.
  • restructure Rust backend into core/ and ffi/ modules
  • Move domain logic from kafka/ to core/ (connection, consumer, producer, types).
  • Convert ffi.rs to ffi/ directory module. kafka/ becomes a re-export shim for
  • backwards compatibility. No logic changes — pure file reorganization.
